description: codex (CLI coding agent) harness + pinch benchmark bundle. Runs codex on a self-hosted runner with the machine login. Pure data — no core changes.

benchmarkRecipes.description: pinch coding-benchmark adapter (template) — turns a jsonl source's repo/ref/test-command into cases. Replace the real pinch's source kind and field names yourself.

datasets.description: PinchBench building-dashboards — coding-agent variant (repo env): the agent writes dashboard.json, and the output is scored by deterministic checks (valid JSON + 4 panel keys). No judge model needed — self-hosted run/scoring with just the codex machine login.

cases.task: Create a file named dashboard.json in the working directory: a valid JSON Axiom-style dashboard for API health, with panels/keys covering p95 latency, p99 latency, error rate, and request volume. Write only that one file.

config.cmd: test -f dashboard.json && python3 -m json.tool dashboard.json >/dev/null && grep -qi p95 dashboard.json && grep -qi p99 dashboard.json && grep -qi error dashboard.json && grep -qi volume dashboard.json

datasets.description: PinchBench (github.com/pinchbench/skill) building-dashboards — the original (prompt env + judge rubric). Requires a judge model. Same benchmark the hermes script uses — for a side-by-side comparison with codex.

cases.task: Using the building-dashboards skill, design an Axiom dashboard for API health: panels for p95/p99 latency, error rate, and request volume. Output the dashboard JSON.

config.rubric: PASS if the output is a valid Axiom dashboard covering p95/p99 latency, error rate, and request volume panels.
